mnemonics.title: FTP Connection Types
mnemonics.content: ACTIVE: Client listens, Server connects | PASSIVE: Server listens, Client connects - Think: Active = Server reaches out, Passive = Client reaches out for data

acronyms.usage: Standard protocol for file transfers over TCP/IP networks
Secure version of FTP with encryption
Secure file transfer protocol running over SSH
FTP command to enter passive mode
FTP command to download a file
FTP command to upload a file

visualTips.visualization: Draw two diagrams: Active - Client opens port, Server connects back (arrow from server to client). Passive - Client connects to Server's second port (arrows from client to server for both). Label clearly showing which side initiates data connection.

Show two parallel lines: top line = Control connection (port 21, persistent). Bottom line = Data connection (port 20, created/destroyed per transfer). Arrows show command-response flow on control line.
Group codes by first digit: 1xx (preliminary), 2xx (success), 3xx (needs more info), 4xx (temporary failure), 5xx (permanent failure). Draw as a pyramid or flowchart.

analogies.analogy: FTP is like a post office: Control connection is like the address system (ensuring mail reaches right place), Data connection is like the actual mail delivery - each package needs a new trip.
Active FTP is like ordering delivery where you give your address and wait for delivery person to come to you. Passive FTP is like going to pick up your order from the store yourself.
